title: French bitcoin evangelical Eric Larchevêque wants his right-wing voice to be part of public debate
subhead: Known to the public as a judge on a television show, the French bitcoin pioneer wants to win over the masses to the cryptocurrencies that made him wealthy. With the 2027 presidential election approaching, this libertarian has embraced increasingly radical ideas.

subhead: The organization added that there was 'no leakage of radioactive materials reported' in the area in central Iran.

title: UNRWA head Philippe Lazzarini's grim assessment: 'Without firm political and financial support, we face a slow death'
subhead: The commissioner-general of the United Nations agency for Palestinian refugees, whose term ends on March 31, voices deep concern for the survival of the underfunded organization that has destabilized by Israeli attacks and disregard for international law.

title: Despite exhaustion and fear of Iranian strikes, Israelis continue to support warring government
subhead: Nearly 80% of the population continues to support Benjamin Netanyahu's government after three weeks of conflict with Iran, fueled by the authorities' repeated announcements of victory.

title: International aid convoy reaches Cuba amid humanitarian crisis
subhead: More than 500 worldwide activists have arrived in Havana to deliver to the Cuban authorities roughly 20 metric tons of food supplies, medical equipment.

title: Vladimir Putin, the conductor of a 'special musical operation' in Europe
subhead: Barred from the West, Valery Gergiev, maestro at the helm of Russia's two leading opera houses and cultural ambassador for Vladimir Putin, is seeking a return to conducting in Europe's major concert halls. In this cultural arena, Moscow is shifting from 'soft power' to 'sharp power.'

title: In Paris, Reza Pahlavi, son of Iran's last shah, builds his network
subhead: Reza Pahlavi has been based in the French capital since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran started, making moves in anticipation of the Islamic Republic's fall.
